name: Signed to Unsigned Conversion Error
description: The product casts a signed primitive to an unsigned primitive, producing unexpected values when the signed value is negative. A negative error-return value converted to unsigned becomes a very large number, which can violate program assumptions and lead to buffer overflows when used for memory sizes or indices.
detection_pattern: Look for TRANSFORM nodes where a signed integer (especially error return codes like -1) is cast to an unsigned type and then flows to STORAGE or STRUCTURAL nodes for buffer allocation or indexing, without a CONTROL node that checks for negative values before the signed-to-unsigned conversion.
